name: Estrela Orion Belt — circular route in Serra da Estrela
name_original: Estrela Orion Belt — percurso circular na Serra da Estrela
region_intro: Jogging around Manteigas (São Pedro) offers diverse terrain within Portugal's Serra da Estrela mountains. The region features significant glacial formations, including the U-shaped Zêzere Glacier Valley, high peaks, and lush river valleys. Running routes often traverse expansive meadows, beech groves, and areas with granite boulders, providing varied landscapes for runners. This area is part of Portugal's largest protected natural park, offering extensive natural environments for outdoor activity. Best jogging routes around Manteigas (São Pedro) The most popular jogging route is Estrela Taurus — circular route in Serra da Estrela, a 20.4 miles (32.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 39 minutes to complete. This challenging route explores the high-altitude landscapes of the Serra da Estrela. Another top favourite among local runners is Estrela Orion Belt — circular route in Serra da Estrela, a difficult 30.5 miles (49.0 km) path. This extensive route offers panoramic views across the mountainous terrain. Local runners also love the Pico da Serra da Estrela loop from Serra da Estrela, a 15.9 miles (25.7 km) trail leading through high mountain passes, often completed in about 4 hours 7 minutes. Jogging around Manteigas (São Pedro) is defined by glacial valleys, high peaks, and river valleys. The network offers options for various ability levels, from challenging mountain runs to more moderate paths. The routes in Manteigas (São Pedro) are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 3 reviews. More than 350 runners have used komoot to explore Manteigas (São Pedro)'s varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails around Manteigas (São Pedro)?
A: The running trails in Manteigas (São Pedro) offer a diverse range of terrains. You'll encounter significant glacial formations, including the U-shaped Zêzere Glacier Valley, high peaks, and lush river valleys. Routes often traverse expansive meadows, serene beech groves, and areas dotted with granite boulders. Some trails feature mostly paved surfaces, while others are more rugged, requiring sturdy shoes and a good footing.

Q: Are there any easy or moderate running routes suitable for all fitness levels?
A: While many routes in Manteigas (São Pedro) are challenging, there are options for various ability levels. The region's extensive trail network includes paths that are suitable for casual jogs, such as those winding through the Zêzere Glacier Valley's meadows. Although the guide lists no 'easy' routes, there are 4 'moderate' routes available, offering a less strenuous experience than the more difficult mountain runs.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in Manteigas (São Pedro)?
A: Yes, Manteigas (São Pedro) offers several circular running routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6910184" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Manteigas&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 25 km trail with over 1,100 meters of elevation gain, providing a comprehensive experience of the area's landscapes. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21950073" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Zêzere Valleys – Manteigas loop from Manteigas&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 27.6 km route offering stunning vistas.

Q: What are some natural landmarks or points of interest I might see while running?
A: Manteigas (São Pedro) is rich in natural beauty. You can run past the magnificent U-shaped Zêzere Glacier Valley, explore the serene Faias Wood, or discover the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6019826" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hidden cascade at Poço do Inferno&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable spots include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4915608" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Long Lake&lt;/a&gt; (Lagoa Comprida), a large glacial reservoir, and the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/374228" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covão dos Conchos Trail&lt;/a&gt;, known for its distinctive water reservoir.

Q: Are there any waterfalls along the running trails in Manteigas (São Pedro)?
A: Absolutely. The region is home to several beautiful waterfalls. The most famous is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6019826" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poço do Inferno&lt;/a&gt;, a dramatic waterfall located in a steep gorge. You might also encounter &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5904227" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poço da Broca de Serapitel&lt;/a&gt; or a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5904568" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;waterfall and natural swimming pool in Parque Natural da Serra da Estrela&lt;/a&gt;, offering refreshing sights during your run.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Manteigas (São Pedro)?
A: The running routes in Manteigas (São Pedro) are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 5.0 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 350 runners have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the breathtaking natural beauty and the diverse challenges offered by the trails.

Q: Is Manteigas (São Pedro) a good destination for trail running events?
A: Yes, Manteigas (São Pedro) is recognized as a premier trail running destination. The existence of events like the Serra da Estrela Trail Running Festival highlights the area's suitability for such activities, offering routes from 10 to 55 kilometers with significant elevation gains, traversing dense pine forests and challenging mountain terrain.

Q: Are there any river beaches or places to cool off after a run?
A: Yes, after an invigorating run, you can find refreshing spots. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/974890" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lapa dos Dinheiros River Beach&lt;/a&gt;, nestled on the banks of the Caniça river, offers crystal-clear waters and ancient chestnut trees, providing a perfect place to relax. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/374228" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covão dos Conchos Trail&lt;/a&gt; also leads to a unique water reservoir where a short swim is possible.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Manteigas (São Pedro)?
A: Manteigas (São Pedro) offers beautiful running conditions throughout much of the year. Autumn is particularly scenic, as the Faias Wood and São Lourenço Beeches transform with vibrant colors, creating an orange carpet of fallen leaves. However, the high-altitude nature of Serra da Estrela means conditions can vary, so checking local weather forecasts is always advisable, especially for winter runs.

Q: How many running routes are available in Manteigas (São Pedro)?
A: There are 15 dedicated running routes available around Manteigas (São Pedro) listed in this guide. These routes offer a range of distances and difficulties, primarily catering to moderate to difficult fitness levels.

Q: What kind of elevation gain can I expect on the running routes?
A: The running routes in Manteigas (São Pedro) often feature significant elevation changes due to the mountainous terrain of the Serra da Estrela. For instance, routes can range from 3 miles with 330 ft of elevation gain for easier paths, to more challenging trails exceeding 10 miles with over 1,000 ft of elevation gain, requiring very good fitness.
tips: At an altitude of 1,700 metres, in the heart of the Serra da Estrela, stands the impressive Nave da Mestra, a granite colossus that holds a centuries-old secret: the Barca Herminius. Built in 1910 by craftsmen from Manteigas, who climbed the mountain with mules and hydraulic jacks, this house nestled between rocks looks like something out of a fairy tale. It is said that it was designed as a summer retreat by the Matos Preto family, but the legend goes further: it is said that clandestine meetings against the regime, led by judge José Pereira de Matos, took place there. That is why, to this day, it is called the Judge's House.
This is the HP of mainland Portugal. The short way up is to drive up to the top, park up and walk 50m to the summit and then go and buy some good cheese and meats from the summit shops!
